channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Is Russia the Main Beneficiary of Trump’s Iran Mistake?
item.description: The Kremlin is gaining billions in additional oil revenue.

item.title: EU Takes Aim at Russia’s Abductions of Ukrainian Children
item.description: Around 20,500 minors have been kidnapped since war broke out in February 2022.

item.title: Mapping the Iran war’s trade disruption
item.description: Petrol prices are rising. So are those of plastics and pistachios

item.title: By one measure, America’s allies now outspend it on defence
item.description: Our annual ranking of military budgets shows the biggest shift since 2001

item.title: A prolonged Iran crisis could irreversibly damage Gulf states
item.description: If there is no deal by the end of the summer, long-term economic harm is likely

item.title: China knows that governing new tech can be harder than inventing it
item.description: You need enough freedom for innovation, but enough control to prevent disaster

item.title: China wants more robots but not fewer workers
item.description: A human-first approach to automation

item.title: Trump Goes to Beijing: What to Watch
item.description: China’s belated confirmation of the visit reflects Beijing’s broader approach to Washington under Trump’s second administration: do not initiate, do not refuse, and do not compromise.

item.title: America is experiencing a productivity miracle
item.description: AI hasn’t—yet—got much to do with it

item.title: The War Room newsletter: Drones are rewiring warfare. Literally
item.description: Shashank Joshi, our defence editor, looks at a pivotal weapon on the battlefield: the fibre-optic drone

item.title: How Africa Can Escape the Debt Trap
item.description: Despite being the least-indebted continent, Africa remains stuck in a debt trap. The trap does not reflect the volume of debt African countries have accumulated—which is just 3% of the global total—but rather how that debt is structured and perceived, which in turn reflects a flawed global financial architecture.

item.title: Iran War Chokes ‘Major Driver’ of Global Economy
item.description: The Hormuz crisis is hitting diesel even harder than crude.

item.title: India’s pricey private universities want to take on the Ivy League
item.description: Billionaire founders are bankrolling their efforts

item.title: Trump Must Put Detained Uyghur Intellectuals on the Agenda for Xi Summit
item.description: If Trump sits across from Xi and fails to raise the cases of Uyghur Americans and their families, Beijing will read that omission not as pragmatism, but as weakness.

item.title: Europe Shrugs Off Trump’s Latest Threats
item.description: Facing U.S. troop withdrawal, the continent’s leaders feel less alarmed and better prepared.

item.title: Overcoming the AI Wisdom Gap
item.description: As AI chatbots become a fixture of daily life, people are increasingly turning to them for emotional support and moral guidance. AI developers must work together with faith communities to ensure that their systems embody the shared values that have long shaped human societies.

item.title: Who Wants Hezbollah to Stay Armed?
item.description: Grievances with the Lebanese state, not sectarian loyalty, are driving popular support for the group retaining its guns.

item.title: Allies at War: Japan-US Relations From the War on Terror to Today’s Iran Conflict
item.description: Insights from Tokyo's handling of the Iraq War could help Japan navigate the current demands from the United States regarding the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: China’s Malacca Dilemma, After Hormuz
item.description: Western-dominated insurance premiums can choke off Beijing’s oil supplies more effectively than warships can.

item.title: Kazakh and Uzbek Leaders Attend Muted Victory Day Parade in Moscow
item.description: In their bilateral meetings, Putin emphasized how grateful he was that the Kazakh and Uzbek leaders came to stand with Russia.

item.title: Interest Rates Can’t Control Today’s Inflation
item.description: Inflation is once again rising across advanced economies, driven by geopolitical turmoil, surging energy prices, and fragile supply chains rather than excess demand. Yet policymakers continue to treat it as a monetary problem, relying on a tool that cannot address the underlying causes.

item.title: Why Is China Watching India-Vietnam Relations Carefully
item.description: China appears to be closely monitoring the development of India-Vietnam relations, particularly in the areas of defense, maritime affairs, and strategic technology.

item.title: The Software Layer of China-US Tech Diplomacy
item.description: When Trump and Xi meet in Beijing, the most consequential AI question on the table will be one neither government’s existing toolkit was built to answer.

item.title: With Farrer Win, One Nation Emerges as Major Threat to the Liberal Party
item.description: The Liberal-National Coalition directed their preferences to a party that could ultimately replace them.

item.title: Iran Does Not Have a Right to Enrich Uranium
item.description: Trump should push for zero enrichment in perpetuity as part of any deal with Tehran.

item.title: Iran Is the Test China Didn’t Ask For
item.description: How China handles this conflict will reveal more about its actual power – and its vulnerabilities – than any trade deal announced in Beijing.

item.title: Lessons for Democrats from a candidate who sings and shoots
item.description: It’s good to be charming and unconventional. It’s really good to be famous

item.title: Who Will Solve the AI Productivity Puzzle?
item.description: Generative AI tools have been widely available for several years now, but productivity growth has little sustained improvement, because firms have yet to convert task-level time savings into measurable economic output. Is the most effective solution to overhaul existing companies—or to bypass them altogether?

item.title: India Still Sees Russia as a Trusted Friend. Is That a Mistake?
item.description: Is India's love for Russia built on nostalgia for a relationship that no longer exists?

item.title: Rethinking Transitional Justice in Bosnia
item.description: Western assessments miss how economic ties have quietly advanced reconciliation.

item.title: Gold’s Grim Message
item.description: Central banks’ purchases and repatriation of gold are on the rise, and both should be viewed as a symptom of deglobalization. They signal the advent of a more geopolitically fragmented world in which cross-border transactions of all kinds are poised to become more difficult and costly.

item.title: Governments Can’t Agree on What AI Actually Is
item.description: Without clear definitions, governance is impossible.

item.title: There’s No Such Thing as Climate Policy
item.description: It’s time to rethink top-down attempts at environmental progress.

item.title: Geospatial Intelligence Expert Y. Nithiyanandam on Why the Hormuz Strait is No Longer a Neutral Global Common
item.description: “Access now depends on where you stand with Tehran, whether you are willing to comply with its rerouting demands, and whether you have quietly negotiated your place in the new order.”

item.title: Aung San Suu Kyi’s Move to House Arrest Is a Calculated Deception
item.description: Myanmar’s military regime wants the world to believe it is changing. It is not.

item.title: Former Thai PM Thaksin Shinawatra Released From Prison on Parole
item.description: Following his party's worst electoral result in more than two decades, it remains to be seen if the veteran politician can mount another political comeback.

item.title: The Rise of Personality Politics in India
item.description: Criticism of the government is interpreted as criticism of a beloved leader. Elections risk turning into emotional plebiscites on leaders rather than informed debates on policy.

item.title: The Stakes of Trump vs. Xi
item.description: How the summit could change the course of U.S.-China competition.

item.title: The Winners and Losers of the Iran Energy Shock
item.description: How the war created a new geopolitical divide.

item.title: The Stakes of Trump vs. Xi
item.description: How the summit could change the course of U.S.-China competition.

item.title: A Case for Judicial Review of the Awami League Ban Under the ATA
item.description: Instrumentalizing anti-terrorism law to achieve a political goal creates a dangerous precedent in any democratic polity.

item.title: A Year After Operation Sindoor, Pakistan Is in Trouble at Home, Basks in Glory Abroad
item.description: Pakistan’s international profile has improved, aided by developments post-Operation Sindoor, its warm ties with Trump, and the churn in global geopolitics.

item.title: ASEAN Leaders Call For Middle East Calm, Reopening of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The region has also pledged to move forward with a fuel-sharing agreement to offset shortages of oil from the Persian Gulf.
